rationale: Restores the two missing PostHog events (APP-1892) with a precise root-cause fix exactly as described. `enterprise/server/routes/auth.py` is extended: the existing `POST /api/complete_onboarding` handler now accepts a typed `OnboardingSubmission` Pydantic body (with a `selections` mapping), fires `analytics.track_onboarding_completed(...)` and an org `posthog.group_identify(...)` to stamp `onboarding_completed_at`, all wrapped in try/except so analytics failures never block onboarding. The stale orphan `enterprise/server/routes/onboarding.py` and its `test_onboarding.py` are deleted, leaving a single source of truth. New `enterprise/server/routes/analytics_events.py` declares `analytics_events_router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/analytics/events', tags=['Analytics'])` with `POST /create-pr-button-clicked` whose payload is `Literal`-constrained on `git_provider` (so arbitrary client strings cannot become PostHog properties) and fires `analytics.track_create_pr_button_clicked(...)`. `enterprise/saas_server.py` adds `from server.routes.analytics_events import analytics_events_router` and `base_app.include_router(analytics_events_router)`. `openhands/analytics/analytics_constants.py` adds the `CREATE_PR_BUTTON_CLICKED` constant (with the deliberate 'spaces' naming convention the PR body flags for reviewer attention), and `openhands/analytics/analytics_service.py` adds the typed `track_create_pr_button_clicked()` method. Frontend: new `use-track-create-pr-button-clicked.ts` mutation hook fired from both `GitControlBarPrButton` and `GitToolsSubmenu`; `analytics-events.api.ts` carries the API client. Tests cover happy path, unauthenticated request, analytics disabled, analytics-exception isolation, `group_identify` skipped when no org, payload validation rejecting unknown providers — including a frontend Vitest test for the mutation hook. The 'errors are deliberately swallowed' callouts are documented best-effort analytics fallbacks (not a cheat).
